summary: Improved matching in src/melee/lb/lb_00CE.c by replacing generated-looking powi exponent handling with a natural loop that matched, and by reshaping lb_8000D148 floating-point temporaries/expression order to improve fuzzy match while preserving stack frame shape.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; contains powi cleanup to a natural exponent loop and lb_8000D148 temporary/expression-order matching changes.

what_changed.text: powi replaced an unrolled/generated-looking exponent block using exp_minus_8, remaining, and powers_done with a simple for loop using i from 0 to exponent, multiplying result by base each iteration.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: match `powi` with a natural exponent loop

what_changed.text: powi was reported as matched by both the PR body checkdiff command and the bot report, with the bot listing powi moving from 81.22% to 100.00%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: 1 new match table for powi

what_changed.text: lb_8000D148 changed local declaration/order and expression staging: introduced a cross temporary, reused dist_01 for squared distance before sqrtf, and split squared-distance calculations into assignment/multiply/add steps.
lb_8000D148 was reported as improved from about 86.3% to about 98.9% fuzzy match; the PR body says this preserved the correct stack frame shape.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: improve `lb_8000D148` from 86.3% to 98.9% fuzzy match while preserving the correct stack frame shape

what_changed.text: Project build verification was reported with `python configure.py --wrapper wine && ninja` producing `build/GALE01/main.dol: OK`.

smart_moves.text: For powi, the author preferred a natural source loop over a complex unrolled block, and verified it as a match rather than preserving generated-looking code.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: match `powi` with a natural exponent loop

smart_moves.text: For lb_8000D148, the author improved fuzzy match through narrow local-expression and temporary-lifetime reshaping without introducing broad data, header, or split changes.
The PR recorded narrow verification for each affected function plus a full build result.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated multiply blocks in exponentiation helpers can be original natural loops; try a simple counter loop and verify before keeping unrolled generated-looking code.
For floating-point geometry code, local temporary staging and expression order can materially affect fuzzy match and stack shape; keep such changes narrow and verify the target function.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: lb_8000D148 fuzzy match and stack frame claim
human_pr_text: `python tools/checkdiff.py lb_8000D148 --format json` -> 98.85714% fuzzy match

naming_conventions.text: powi simplified temporary naming from generated/algorithm-fragment names such as exp_minus_8, remaining, and powers_done to a conventional loop counter i after the natural loop form was recovered.

lb_8000D148 uses coordinate-difference and squared-distance local names such as diff_01_x, diff_02_y, dist_squared_02, and threshold_squared; the PR preserves this descriptive geometric naming while changing declaration and assignment order.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace unrolled exponent multiplication with a natural `for (; i < exponent; i++) result *= base;` loop.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.when_to_try: When exponentiation code appears as repeated multiply blocks or power counters and the function can be checked directly.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust float temporary lifetimes and assignment order to match codegen, such as computing a cross-product term separately and splitting squared-distance expressions into multiply then add steps.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.when_to_try: When a floating-point geometry function is close but fuzzy mismatch suggests expression scheduling or stack-frame/local lifetime differences.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: lb_8000D148 98.85714% fuzzy match and stack frame shape

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use narrow checkdiff commands for each target symbol and record both exact match and fuzzy-match percentages.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.when_to_try: For small PRs touching one translation unit or a few functions.

follow_up_queries: powi natural exponent loop checkdiff match
lb_8000D148 fuzzy match stack frame shape

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, author, dates, changed files
raw_diff: complete diff for the one changed file
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report with match percentages
notes: No human review comments were present in the loaded slice; review_feedback is intentionally empty.
The PR body reports stack frame shape preservation for lb_8000D148, but the slice does not include objdiff JSON details beyond the reported fuzzy percentage.
Verification commands are reported by the PR body and bot comment; this intake did not rerun builds or objdiff.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Natural exponent loop matched powi
accepted_candidate_records.text: In src/melee/lb/lb_00CE.c, PR #2505 replaced powi's generated-looking unrolled exponent handling with a simple counter loop multiplying result by base; reported verification was `python tools/checkdiff.py powi` -> match and bot report showed powi at 100.00%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Float expression staging improved lb_8000D148 fuzzy match
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2505 improved lb_8000D148 fuzzy match by changing temporary declaration order and staging float expressions, including a separate cross temporary and split squared-distance calculations; reported result was about 98.86% fuzzy match.

source_update_candidates.title: Add powi as historical example for natural-loops
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2505 is a concrete example where replacing an unrolled exponentiation block with a natural for-loop matched powi. This could be referenced as an example under the existing natural-loops standard, not as a new rule.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: match `powi` with a natural exponent loop
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded global standard already covers natural loops; this PR provides a supported historical example for curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a general rule that all exponentiation helpers should use a loop; this PR only proves powi in lb_00CE with reported checkdiff evidence.
Do not promote lb_8000D148's specific temporary order as a global style rule; evidence supports it as a local matching tactic for this function.
Do not claim human reviewer approval details beyond merge status; the loaded slice contains no review comments.
